title: AI Agents: Unlocking Long-Term Autonomy & Realizing Value Now! #shorts
summary: New AI models are claimed to enable long-horizon autonomous behavior in agents. The post predicts significant business value will be unlocked as AI agents assume greater responsibility for complex tasks.
writeup: This short from Cyril Imhof argues that new AI models enable long-horizon autonomous behavior in agents, allowing them to take on complex tasks over extended timeframes. The framing connects this capability to delegation: as agents assume greater responsibility, more work can be handed off rather than directly supervised. The central claim is that this shift will unlock significant business value as agents take on more complex tasks.
